title: Dashboard E1 — Bearer auth for LAN access
description: Extend createFabricHttpApp to optionally accept a Bearer token for authenticated LAN access. When a bearer token is provided, mount the SDK's requireBearerAuth middleware from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/auth before all API and MCP routes. When no token provided, default to localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1). Add --auth-token flag to fab serve CLI and a --host flag (default '127.0.0.1', set to '0.0.0.0' for LAN).

files.change: Add optional authToken param to createFabricHttpApp options; if authToken provided: mount requireBearerAuth middleware from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/auth on all routes; bind to options.host (default '127.0.0.1'); if no authToken: keep localhost-only binding

Add Commander options: --host <host> (default '127.0.0.1'), --auth-token <token> (optional); pass to startHttpServer options; print warning if --host 0.0.0.0 used without --auth-token: 'Warning: LAN access without auth token — consider using --auth-token'

implementation: Update createFabricHttpApp to accept optional { authToken?: string, host?: string } in options
Mount requireBearerAuth middleware conditionally if authToken is set
Update startHttpServer and serve.ts to pass through host and authToken from CLI flags
Test: curl without auth token to LAN host should return 401; curl with correct Bearer token should succeed

reference.examples: Existing createFabricHttpApp options pattern; SDK auth .d.ts for requireBearerAuth API

criteria: fab serve --host 0.0.0.0 --auth-token mytoken — curl from another machine with Authorization: Bearer mytoken succeeds
fab serve --host 0.0.0.0 --auth-token mytoken — curl without auth header returns HTTP 401
fab serve (no flags) — still binds to 127.0.0.1 only (localhost-only default preserved)
fab serve --host 0.0.0.0 (without auth-token) — warning message printed to stderr

rationale.chosen_approach: Use SDK's built-in requireBearerAuth middleware for consistency with SDK's auth model; keep localhost-only default for zero-config safety
alternatives_considered: Custom JWT middleware (adds dependency, reinvents SDK's auth)
decision_factors: SDK auth middleware is already available as transitive dep
LAN sharing is an extension — default localhost behavior must not change
rationale.tradeoffs: Token is passed as CLI flag (visible in process list); for higher security, consider env var. Acceptable for local development tool.

manual_checks: fab serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 7373 --auth-token test123 -- then curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer test123' http://127.0.0.1:7373/api/rules
Same setup without header — verify 401 response
fab serve with no flags — verify binds to 127.0.0.1

risks.description: requireBearerAuth SDK signature may differ from expectation
risks.mitigation: Read actual .d.ts file before implementing; fall back to custom express middleware if SDK auth API is too complex
risks.fallback: Implement simple custom Bearer token check middleware: req.headers.authorization === 'Bearer {token}' ? next() : res.status(401).json({error:'Unauthorized'})
